storylines.moment: Bombing near Macron's Damascus hotel
storylines.why_now: Explosives detonated near the Four Seasons hotel where the French president was staying during his Syria state visit.

storylines.moment: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body
storylines.why_now: A major structural shift in Gaza's post-war governance, announced this week.

storylines.moment: US-Iran strikes enter second day — 90 targets hit, Iran retaliates at US bases
storylines.why_now: After Trump declared the ceasefire "over" from the NATO summit in Ankara, the US completed a second round of strikes hitting 90 military targets along Iran's coastline. Iran's Revolutionary Guards struck back at US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

history.note: Trump declares ceasefire 'over' at NATO summit; fresh US strikes launched after Iran targeted ships in Strait of Hormuz
US hits 90 targets across Iran's coastline; Iran retaliates against US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain

storylines.moment: Russia launches mass missile and drone attack on Ukraine
storylines.why_now: Waves of missiles and drones killed at least 22 people, exposing gaps in Ukraine's air defenses more than four years into the war.

storylines.moment: Marine Le Pen announces presidential run despite ankle monitor
storylines.why_now: The far-right leader said she'll run for the French presidency next year even after being sentenced to wear an electronic monitor for embezzlement — a first for a major French candidate.

storylines.moment: Pressure mounts on Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner
storylines.why_now: Facing calls to drop his 2026 bid as new allegations emerge.

storylines.moment: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 3,500
storylines.why_now: Survivors are still searching for missing family members as the toll continues to rise.

storylines.moment: Manhattan high-rise evacuated over collapse fears
storylines.why_now: A 37-story building under construction was evacuated after columns buckled and floors sagged between the 21st and 26th floors.

storylines.moment: Markets extend losses as US-Iran conflict deepens into day two
storylines.why_now: The Dow fell over 1% and Brent crude jumped nearly 6% Tuesday; selling continued Wednesday as strikes and Iranian retaliation showed no sign of de-escalation.

history.note: Dow drops 1%+, Brent crude surges 5.7% on Trump's ceasefire announcement
Losses extend as conflict enters day two; Dow -1.09%, crude up ~6%

storylines.moment: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in biggest Xbox restructuring ever
storylines.why_now: 3,200 of the cuts hit Xbox specifically — about 20% of the gaming division — and four studios including Double Fine and Ninja Theory are being spun off or sold. Xbox CEO cited margins 3-10x below comparable platforms.

storylines.moment: SpaceX joins the Nasdaq-100
storylines.why_now: The privately held rocket company's addition to one of Wall Street's most-watched indexes is a structural landmark for private-market firms seeking public-market legitimacy.

storylines.moment: Blue Origin valued at $130 billion
storylines.why_now: Bezos's space company closed its first outside fundraising round at that valuation.

storylines.moment: Manhattan office leasing hits a 20-year high
storylines.why_now: A sharp counter-signal to years of "office apocalypse" narratives about downtowns emptying out for good.

storylines.moment: OpenAI publicly releases GPT-5.6
storylines.why_now: Ends government-requested limits that had constrained the model's availability.

storylines.moment: $30 million orbital debris salvage mission launches
storylines.why_now: A robotic "lifesaver" is heading up this week to retrieve stranded hardware in orbit.

storylines.moment: Hayabusa2's "snowman" asteroid photos
storylines.why_now: JAXA's probe revealed a near-Earth asteroid shaped unlike anything scientists expected.

storylines.moment: JWST's fourth anniversary images released
storylines.why_now: A familiar galaxy rendered "far richer" than in any prior image of it.

storylines.moment: Tianwen-2 reaches asteroid Kamo'oalewa
storylines.why_now: China's probe arrives after a journey of more than 1 billion kilometers.

storylines.moment: Canadian Artemis II astronaut retires
storylines.why_now: Steps down shortly after the historic crewed lunar flyby earlier this year.

storylines.moment: Solar flares raise aurora odds across the U.S.
storylines.why_now: Three flares this week widen the typical latitude where the northern lights are visible.

storylines.moment: World Cup quarterfinals begin — France-Morocco leads the slate
storylines.why_now: Four quarterfinals kick off today and tomorrow: France vs Morocco, Belgium vs Spain, Norway vs England, and Argentina vs Switzerland. The last eight teams play for semifinal spots.

storylines.moment: Egypt FA files official protest over Argentina refereeing
storylines.why_now: After Argentina's stoppage-time 3-2 comeback win from 2-0 down, Egypt's football association formally called out "controversial and influential refereeing incidents" — adding a political edge to one of the tournament's most dramatic results.

history.note: Egyptian FA officially protests refereeing decisions with FIFA

storylines.moment: World Cup Round of 16 concludes
storylines.why_now: Brazil–Norway, Mexico–England, and Colombia–Switzerland decided the last quarterfinal spots.

storylines.moment: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry at Madison Square Garden
storylines.why_now: After weeks of speculation about the rollout being its own PR campaign, the wedding actually happened — 1,000 guests, Madison Square Garden, and now the post-wedding coverage cycle begins.

history.note: Theory spreads that the multimillion-dollar rollout is itself a PR campaign
Wedding confirmed at Madison Square Garden; 1,000 guests; Blake Lively notably absent

storylines.moment: Blake Lively excluded from the Swift-Kelce wedding
storylines.why_now: With the wedding now confirmed, the reported exclusion is no longer speculation — it's the final chapter of a once-unshakeable Hollywood friendship.

history.note: Reported as the final straw in a friendship once called Hollywood's most bulletproof
Wedding confirmed; Lively absence now documented fact, not rumor

storylines.moment: Beyoncé drops first new song in two years on July 4th
storylines.why_now: "MORNING DEW (DONK)" arrived with no advance notice on the Fourth of July, reigniting the cultural conversation about what a surprise Beyoncé release still does to the internet.

storylines.moment: Rare early Declaration of Independence copy found
storylines.why_now: A volunteer at Britain's National Archives discovered it during America's 250th-anniversary year.

storylines.moment: Prince Harry loses UK press-intrusion lawsuit
storylines.why_now: A British judge ruled against Harry and others in the case against the Daily Mail's publisher.

storylines.moment: Love Island USA backlash cycle
storylines.why_now: A contestant's apology tour has become its own referendum on parasocial reality-TV culture.

storylines.moment: BET Awards live-mic takeover
storylines.why_now: A hosting incident sparked a multiplatform debate about award-show culture.

storylines.moment: Empire State Building antenna climbers get engaged, then arrested
storylines.why_now: Two rooftoppers scaled the antenna and unfurled a pro-peace banner before the proposal — and the arrest.

storylines.moment: 130 people form a human tower to welcome Pope Leo XIV in Barcelona
storylines.why_now: Catalans deployed their traditional "castell" form to greet the new pontiff — a welcome so local it became a global image.
